steps.title: Use Chen's standard for strength
steps.content: Chen says the strongest judicial opinions consider and rebut potential objections. Philosophers who disagree with a judge's view can supply exactly those objections.

steps.title: Reject claims outside the argument
steps.content:
• Choice A: Discussing conflicting philosophers requires consulting philosophical works, not avoiding them.
  • Choice C: The passage does not discuss making opinions easier for nonexperts to understand.
  • Choice D: Chen is not trying to align judges with majority philosophical views.

steps.title: Connect objections to stronger reasoning
steps.content: If judges engage with opposing philosophical views and rebut them, their opinions can present stronger arguments. The answer is B.
